[CUR-982] Fix cross-session auth collision causing wrong role display (#459)

## Summary
- Detect cross-tab Firebase auth collision by tracking the session UID
from `signIn()` in a new `_sessionUid` field
- When `authStateChanges` fires with a different UID (another browser
tab signed in), sign out instead of silently adopting the foreign user's
identity
- Prevents wrong role badge display and addresses FDA 21 CFR Part 11
authority check concerns

## Changes
- **`auth_service.dart`**: Added `_sessionUid` field, set on sign-in,
cleared on sign-out. `_init()` listener compares incoming UID against
`_sessionUid` and calls `signOut()` on mismatch.
- **`auth_service_test.dart`**: New test group simulating cross-tab
collision via `MockFirebaseAuth.stateChangedStreamController`

## Test plan
- [x] New unit test: pushes a different `MockUser` UID onto
`authStateChanges` stream while Admin is signed in → verifies
`isAuthenticated` becomes false and `currentUser` is null
- [x] All 52 existing auth service tests pass
- [ ] Manual: open two browser windows, sign in as Admin in one and
Study Coordinator in the other — verify the first window signs out
instead of showing wrong role

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

feat: rewrite Spotify API to use GraphQL Partner API

The spclient approach (apresolve.spotify.com) returned 404 on ALL endpoints.
The public REST API (api.spotify.com/v1) permanently 429-blocks sp_dc tokens.

Switch to the GraphQL Partner API (api-partner.spotify.com/pathfinder/v1/query)
which is the same backend the official Spotify web player uses. This is the
ONLY confirmed working approach for sp_dc-derived access tokens.

Changes:
- SpotifyAuthConfig: add GraphQL endpoint, client version, persisted query hashes
- SpotifyAuthManager: add getClientToken() for client token acquisition, pack
  clientId into ServiceToken.scope alongside username
- TokenManager: add getSpotifyClientId() interface method
- TokenManagerImpl: implement getSpotifyClientId(), update scope parsing for
  new "username|clientId" format
- SpotifyApiClient: complete rewrite from spclient to GraphQL Partner API
  using libraryV3 and fetchPlaylist persisted queries
- SpotifyDataModule: inject SpotifyAuthManager into SpotifyApiClient

Update Steep v1.10.0 and fix type errors (#348)

* [WIP] Upgrade steep

- `bundle update steep`
- rm rbs files that are included in core rbs
- Comment out some signatures conflicting with core rbs

* Remove RBS definitions that duplicate core RBS (rbs-3.10.3)

After upgrading Steep from 1.8.3 to 1.10.0 (and RBS from 3.6.1 to
3.10.3), the project's custom RBS files that redefine core Ruby
class methods conflict with core RBS definitions. This caused
RBS::DuplicatedMethodDefinitionError and RBS::SuperclassMismatchError,
putting Steep's SignatureService into AncestorErrorStatus and silently
skipping ALL Ruby source file type checking.

Removed duplicated definitions from 13 RBS files:
- buffer.rbs: Array#insert
- data.rbs: Data.define, Data.new, Data#members, Data#to_h
- dir.rbs: Dir.chdir, Dir.delete, Dir.entries, Dir.exist?, Dir.foreach,
  Dir.getwd, Dir.glob, Dir.mkdir, Dir.open, Dir#close, Dir#each,
  Dir#read, Dir#rewind, Dir#seek, Dir#tell, and related aliases
- eval.rbs: Kernel#eval
- file.rbs: File#mtime
- io-console.rbs: IO#read_nonblock, IO::EAGAINWaitReadable class
- kernel.rbs: Kernel#system, Kernel#gets
- metaprog.rbs: Kernel#caller, RbConfig.ruby
- numeric-ext.rbs: Float#floor, Float#ceil, Float#round
- pack.rbs: Array#pack, String#unpack
- signal.rbs: Signal.trap, Signal.list
- string.rbs: String#rindex, String#gsub
- sqlite3.rbs: Time::TimeMethods replaced with untyped

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove unnecessary &block and perm args from File.new's super calls

IO.new does not accept a block or perm parameter, so passing them
from File.new was incorrect. Remove the &block parameter from
File.new's signature and stop passing perm to super.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ✅️ Restore PicoRuby-specific RBS definitions, removing core RBS duplicates

After removing RBS definitions that duplicate core RBS (rbs-3.10.3),
some PicoRuby-specific definitions were also accidentally removed.

ENVClass is defined as `RBS::Unnamed::ENVClass` in the rbs gem because
CRuby's ENV object is an instance of Object. PicoRuby, however, defines
`::ENVClass` as an actual class. While we could alias it via
`class ::ENVClass = ::RBS::Unnamed::ENVClass`, the available methods
differ between CRuby and PicoRuby, so defining it separately is cleaner.

Initially restored kernel.rbs with `system` in the Kernel module and
require.rbs with full LoadError/require/load/require_relative definitions.
However, `system` belongs in Object class (not Kernel module) and
core-standard definitions (LoadError, require, load, require_relative)
should not be redefined. The final state keeps only PicoRuby-specific
additions: private helper methods in require.rbs and system in Object.

* 🙋🏯 Fix all Ruby::NoMethod errors reported by steep check

Address 39 NoMethod errors across 6 categories:

1. Interface type issues (_ToStr, _ToInt, _ToPath): Added `# @type var`
   annotations to narrow interface types to concrete types in File,
   Array#insert, Float#round/ceil, String#gsub, Marshal.load, and
   Kernel#system. The rbs gem defines many methods with interface types
   like `_ToStr`, but PicoRuby accepts concrete types like `String`.
   Rather than overriding the rbs gem definitions (which is difficult —
   instance methods could be overridden via prepend, but singleton
   methods cannot since RBS doesn't define singleton prepend), we add
   type annotations in the method bodies instead.

2. Block type annotation: Kept block.call pattern (instead of converting
   to yield) and added `# @type var block` annotations to specify the
   block parameter types for Dir#each and File#each_line. Initially
   tried replacing block.call with yield for type inference, but this
   changes the method interface unnecessarily.

3. define_method self type: Added `# @type self:` annotations in
   Component HTML tag methods and Model attribute setters.

4. StyleValue#| RBS: Removed `untyped` from the argument type union
   since the concrete types already cover all cases. Initially added
   `untyped` but it weakens type safety unnecessarily.

5. caller nil safety: Used `|| raise` for caller() return values
   in Picotest::Double#method_missing and Test#report to assert
   non-nil. PicoRuby's Kernel#caller never returns nil, so this is
   safe. Initially tried safe navigation (&.) but `|| raise` is more
   explicit about the invariant.

6. VFS File#putc: Fixed `case ch.class` (incorrect pattern) to
   `case ch` for proper case-when type narrowing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* 🙋🏯 Fix Ruby::UnannotatedEmptyCollection and Ruby::AnnotationSyntaxError

Add @type var annotations to empty arrays and hashes across the codebase
to satisfy Steep's UnannotatedEmptyCollection check. Also update mrubyc
submodule reference for a related type annotation fix.

Initially used inline annotation syntax `({} #: Hash[String, untyped])`
for front_matter in markdown.rb, but this caused AnnotationSyntaxError.
Changed to `@type var` annotation style instead.

For instance variables assigned with empty arrays/hashes (e.g.
`@header = {}`, `@footnotes = {}`), use RBS instance variable
declarations (`@ivar: Type`) instead of inline `@type var` annotations,
since `@type var` does not work for instance variables.

Remove ADC::EMPTY_PARAMS constant workaround. The constant was introduced
to avoid UnannotatedEmptyCollection on the default argument `{}`.
This appears to be a Steep bug: UnannotatedEmptyCollection is reported
when the parameter type alias resolves to `Hash[untyped, untyped]`,
but the warning is suppressed when a concrete type like
`Hash[String, untyped]` is used. Use `steep:ignore` as a workaround.

Also fix nil safety for String#[] return values in http_request.rb
using `or raise` pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ✅️ Fix Ruby::MethodParameterMismatch by converting self.new to initialize in RBS

RBS auto-generates self.new type from initialize definition, so having
both causes MethodParameterMismatch. Converted self.new definitions
to initialize across RBS files with return type void.

Keep self.new in RBS for classes that explicitly define `def self.new`
in Ruby (SPI, MbedTLS::CMAC) or define `new` via mrbc_define_method
in C (FAT::Dir, FAT::File, VRAM, Sandbox, MbedTLS::Digest,
MbedTLS::HMAC, MbedTLS::PKey::RSA, MbedTLS::Cipher, KeyboardMatrix,
SQLite3::Statement, ENVClass, SSLSocket, SSLContext, TCPSocket,
TCPServer, UDPSocket).

Also fixed:

- Added Object#open RBS for PicoRuby's simplified Kernel#open

* ✅️ Add type annotations to narrow interface types to concrete types

Core RBS uses interface types (::string, ::path, ::int, etc.) for
standard library methods like File.chmod, File.exist?, Dir.mkdir, etc.
However, PicoRuby's VFS/Dir implementations accept only concrete types
(String, Integer, Time). Since we cannot change the core RBS definitions,
we add `# @type var` annotations in the Ruby method bodies to narrow
the interface types to concrete types before passing them to VFS methods.

Affected files:
- picoruby-vfs/mrblib/file.rb: chmod, open, exist?, directory?, file?,
  unlink, rename, utime, contiguous?, load_file, initialize
- picoruby-vfs/mrblib/dir.rb: exist?, empty?, chdir, mkdir, unlink,
  initialize
- picoruby-posix-io/mrblib/0_io.rb: popen
- picoruby-posix-io/mrblib/1_file.rb: expand_path, foreach
- picoruby-pack/mrblib/pack.rb: pack, unpack

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* 🙋🏹💎 Add nil guards for String|nil to String type mismatches

When String#[] or String#byteslice returns String?, the nil case needs
explicit handling. Depending on context:
- Use `or raise` when nil is logically unreachable (e.g., slicing with
  valid indices after bounds checking)
- Use `.to_s` when nil should become empty string
- Extract instance variables to local variables to enable flow-sensitive
  type narrowing (e.g., @locked_layer in keyboard.rb)

Also fixed a subtle bug in ble_advertising_report.rb where `value&.empty?`
would not break on nil (safe navigation returns nil which is falsy),
changed to `value.nil? || value.empty?` for correct nil handling.

Fix type

* ✅️ Fix Dir type errors: steep:ignore for class << self bug, fix findnext RBS

- Add steep:ignore ArgumentTypeMismatch with bug description comment to
  Dir.open in both picoruby-dir and picoruby-vfs, where `class << self`
  causes steep to resolve `instance` type as `singleton(Dir)` instead of `Dir`.
- Fix Dir#findnext RBS return type from `Dir` to `String?` to match actual
  implementation which returns a filename string or nil.

Reproduction of the steep bug:

  class Foo
    class << self
      def bar(&block)
        f = self.new
        block.call(f) # steep resolves `f` as `singleton(Foo)` instead of `Foo`
      end
    end
  end

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ✅️ Suppress UnexpectedPositionalArgument/UnexpectedBlockGiven in File.new

Core RBS defines IO.new as `(int fd) -> void` without accepting mode
or block parameters. PicoRuby's File.new passes these to super, which
is valid at runtime but incompatible with the core RBS signature.

* 💎 Fix NoMethod errors in rapicco parser and miscellaneous type fixes

Fix type errors in rapicco parser where Hash values (String|Integer|nil)
were used with methods like to_sym/to_i without type narrowing.

Initially used safe navigation operator (&.to_sym, &.to_i) and removed
has_key? guards, but this changed the semantics when keys are missing.
Changed to .to_s.to_sym / .to_s.to_i with has_key? guards to properly
narrow the types while preserving original behavior.

Also includes fixes for: cable.rbs union types,
picotest.rbs declarations, kernel block annotation,
mml.rb ArgumentTypeMismatch, yaml.rb type widening.

* 🏯 Fix UnresolvedOverloading for Integer#* in cable.rb

Integer#** returns Integer|Float|Rational|Complex per core RBS
overloads, which makes Integer#* unable to resolve the overload.
Annotate the intermediate result as Integer since both operands are
Integer in this context.

* ✅️ Fix ReturnTypeMismatch errors in net.rb, kernel.rb, and 1_file.rb

- net.rbs: Change header_t from Hash[String, String] to
  Hash[String, String | nil] since HTTP header values from split may be nil
- kernel.rb: steep:ignore for Kernel#p single-arg return which doesn't
  match core RBS type parameter resolution
- 1_file.rb: steep:ignore for File.foreach without block returning File
  instead of core RBS expected Enumerator (PicoRuby-specific behavior)

* ✅️ Suppress BlockBodyTypeMismatch for File.open block return types

File.open blocks in load_file and foreach return String/File values
that don't match core RBS block type expectations. This is a
fundamental difference between PicoRuby's File.open and the core RBS
definition.

* 💎 Fix MethodBodyTypeMismatch errors in Dir.open, File.open, and each_line

- Dir.open (picoruby-dir, picoruby-vfs) and File.open (picoruby-vfs):
  steep:ignore for `class << self` bug where `instance` type resolves
  to `singleton(Class)` instead of the actual instance type
  https://github.com/soutaro/steep/issues/2121
- File#each_line (picoruby-vfs): Return self at end of method to match
  core RBS return type `self | Enumerator[String, self]`. The original
  code did not return a meaningful value, so this behavior change aligns
  with CRuby's semantics without breaking existing callers.

* ✅️ Suppress BlockTypeMismatch in Kernel#open File.open call

The IO block type (^(IO) -> T | nil) is not compatible with File.open's
block type (^(File) -> T) when passed via &block. This is inherent to
Kernel#open bridging IO and File APIs.

* 🏯 Fix Funicular component type errors in normalize_vnode

Change normalize_vnode return type from child_t to concrete VNode
subclass types (VElement|VText|VComponent|VFragment|nil), since the
method never actually returns String or other child_t members. Using
child_t caused type errors in callers that don't accept all child_t
members.

Cast VNode case branch value to untyped, since the abstract VNode class
cannot be directly returned as a concrete type. Its subclasses always
match one of the listed concrete return types, so this is safe.

* ✅️ Suppress MethodArityMismatch errors with steep:ignore comments

PicoRuby's simplified method implementations have fewer parameters
than core CRuby RBS declarations. Rather than adding unused parameters
to match the core signatures, suppress the errors with steep:ignore
to keep the original interfaces intact.

Affected methods: Dir.entries, Dir.foreach, Dir.open, Dir.glob,
IO.open, IO.popen, IO.pipe, IO#each, Kernel#gets, Kernel#open,
Kernel#system, Kernel#load, GC.start, Marshal.dump, Marshal.load,
Array#pack, String#unpack, String#rindex, Float#round,
File.open, File.foreach, File#initialize, File#each_line, File#gets
(across picoruby-dir, picoruby-vfs, picoruby-posix-io, and others)

It also suppresses `MethodParameterMismatch`
